title: [lora_l2.c:802-817] lichen_lora_l2_tx: -EINVAL for zero length but doc doesn't mention it
description:
Contract violation: documentation

**Issue:** The function returns -EINVAL when len==0 (line 810) but the header documentation doesn't mention this error case.

**Header doc (lora_l2.h:168-170):**
```
 * @param len Length of data (max 255 bytes)
 * @return 0 on success, negative errno on failure
```

**Implementation:**
```c
if (len == 0) {
    LOG_ERR("TX with zero length");
    return -EINVAL;
}
```

**Impact:** Minor - callers won't know zero-length TX is rejected without reading implementation.

**Fix:** Add to doc: '@return -EINVAL if data is NULL or len is 0'
close_reason: Fixed: documented -EINVAL for data=NULL or len=0, and all other return values
